title: rosa glauca var. friesii
intro: Rosa glauca var. friesii, also known as the Scotch rose, is a shrub species of the rose family, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and fragrant, pale yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rosa glauca var. friesii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for a range of applications in natural medicine.
